title: No FabricService (FastAPI Gateway App)
description: No FastAPI app that combines FastAPIProtocolRouter + FabricHealthEndpoints into a runnable Gateway Mode service with endpoints: POST /send, /receive, /stream, /close_session, GET /live, /ready, /health
impact: Gateway Mode cannot run — one of two main deployment modes completely missing
proposed_solution: Create src/application/fabric_service.py that creates a FastAPI() app, includes FastAPIProtocolRouter (with /send, /receive, /stream, /close_session routes) and FabricHealthEndpoints (with /live, /ready, /health). Wire it into main.py as an optional entry-point.
evidence: src/application/fastapi_router.py has FastAPIProtocolRouter with /v1/{path}, /v1/graphql, /v1/webhooks/{event_type}, /v1/stream but NO FastAPI app wraps it. src/wiring/adapters/fabric_health.py has FabricHealthEndpoints with /live, /ready, /health but is not wired into any FastAPI app. .docs/usage_integration_examples/tests/test_fabric_integration.py assumes a running FabricService at localhost:8000.
notes: Replace GWY-001 scope: individual building blocks (FastAPIProtocolRouter, FabricHealthEndpoints) DO exist, but the aggregating FastAPI application is missing.

title: No RemoteGatewayAdapter in src/
description: RemoteGatewayAdapter (httpx.AsyncClient-based ICommunicationGateway) exists only in .docs/usage_integration_examples/gateway_mode/remote_adapter.py, not in src/
impact: Gateway Mode cannot run — actor process has no way to connect to remote FabricService
proposed_solution: Copy .docs/usage_integration_examples/gateway_mode/remote_adapter.py -> src/application/remote_adapter.py; add liveness_check/readiness_check to ICommunicationGateway contract
evidence: gateway_mode/remote_adapter.py imports from contracts.communication_gateway but .docs is NOT importable from src/ without sys.path manipulation
notes: Also missing: liveness_check() and readiness_check() are called by RemoteGatewayAdapter but NOT defined in ICommunicationGateway protocol

title: No Client SDK Package
description: No pip-installable package (pyproject.toml/setup.py) for FabricClient, ICommunicationGateway, or RemoteGatewayAdapter
impact: Every fabric user must copy-paste contracts and adapters manually; imports from .docs/break unless sys.path is manipulated
proposed_solution: Create pyproject.toml with fabric-sdk package; export ICommunicationGateway, FabricClient, RemoteGatewayAdapter as public API; add proper __init__.py exports
evidence: No pyproject.toml, setup.py, setup.cfg, or requirements.txt found anywhere in the project. README instructs 'pip install -r requirements.txt' which does not exist.

title: No Formal API Versioning Scheme
description: No documented versioning strategy; /v1 prefix exists ad-hoc in FastAPIProtocolRouter but there is no version policy, no Accept-Version header, and no deprecation/ migration docs
impact: Once FabricService goes to production, any contract change breaks all connected clients without warning
proposed_solution: Define versioning strategy doc; add /api/v1 prefix consistently; add optional Accept-Version header support; document breaking-change policy in ARCHITECTURE.md or equivalent
evidence: src/application/fastapi_router.py uses prefix='/v1' by default (line 65). gateway_mode/actor_service/main.py uses http://fabric-service:8000/api/v1. No formal version enforcement exists.

title: No OpenAPI API Documentation
description: No auto-generated OpenAPI docs because no FastAPI() app exists to aggregate routers; even individual routers lack Pydantic response models
impact: Adopting Gateway Mode requires reading source code directly — no interactive API docs
proposed_solution: Create FastAPI app in fabric_service.py with include_router; add Pydantic request/response models for each endpoint; enable FastAPI auto-generated /docs and /redoc
evidence: FastAPIProtocolRouter returns Response/StreamingResponse/JSONResponse without Pydantic models. FabricHealthEndpoints returns dicts without Pydantic models.

title: No Client-Side Retry or Circuit Breaking in RemoteGatewayAdapter
description: RemoteGatewayAdapter (.docs only) has timeout=30.0 but zero retry logic; L4 RetryEngine and AsyncCircuitBreaker exist in src/ but are internal mechanisms, not exposed to the SDK client
impact: If FabricService is busy or briefly unavailable, the actor fails immediately without resilience
proposed_solution: Add RetryDecorator or httpx mounts with retry transport to RemoteGatewayAdapter; optionally expose AsyncCircuitBreaker for gateway connection health
evidence: src/transport/retry.py has RetryEngine with exponential backoff. src/network/resilience/circuit_breaker.py has AsyncCircuitBreaker. Neither is used by RemoteGatewayAdapter (.docs).
notes: Internal retry/breaker are L4-L5 mechanisms; gateway SDK needs its own client-side resilience layer

title: src/main.py missing AppRunner import
description: src/main.py line 23 calls 'await AppRunner.run(config_file)' but AppRunner is never imported — this is a runtime NameError waiting to happen
impact: Cannot run python src/main.py — entry point is broken by design
proposed_solution: Add 'from wiring.runner import AppRunner' to src/main.py imports
evidence: src/main.py:23 uses AppRunner, but there is no import statement for it (verified via grep — zero matches for 'from wiring' or 'import.*AppRunner' in main.py)
notes: This is a blocking bug in the composition root

title: No src/contracts/ directory — import path mismatch
description: README, conftest.py, and usage examples import from 'contracts.communication_gateway' but actual file is at 'src/wiring/contracts/communication_gateway.py'. SDK consumers cannot import without knowledge of internal layout.
impact: Consumers get ImportError; the public API path is undocumented and inconsistent with the codebase's own tests and docs
proposed_solution: Either (a) add src/contracts/__init__.py with re-exports from wiring.contracts, or (b) update all .docs references to use 'from wiring.contracts.communication_gateway import ICommunicationGateway'
evidence: conftest.py:14: 'from contracts.communication_gateway import ICommunicationGateway'. README docs show top-level contracts/ dir. But actual path is src/wiring/contracts/
notes: The .docs/ directory works via implicit sys.path but src/ does not have this re-export

title: ICommunicationGateway protocol missing liveness_check/readiness_check
description: ICommunicationGateway defines only health_check(). Both FabricHealthEndpoints and RemoteGatewayAdapter call liveness_check() and readiness_check() which are not in the protocol contract
impact: Gateway health probes and remote adapter break structural subtyping; any ICommunicationGateway implementation must add extra methods without contract enforcement
proposed_solution: Add 'async def liveness_check() -> Dict[str, Any]: ...' and 'async def readiness_check() -> Dict[str, Any]: ...' to ICommunicationGateway Protocol class
evidence: src/wiring/adapters/fabric_health.py:56-62 calls self.gateway.liveness_check() and self.gateway.readiness_check(). src/wiring/contracts/communication_gateway.py:45 only defines health_check. .docs/gateway_mode/remote_adapter.py:50-51 implements liveness_check/readiness_check outside the protocol.
